commitMessage:
fix: decompose subtasks now assign correct project per subtask

Previously all subtasks were filed under the parent idea's project,
causing frontend subtasks to land in the wrong repo. Now:
- decompose_idea receives the full project list and includes it in the prompt
- LLM must assign an exact project_name per subtask based on which repo the work belongs to
- project_name is validated against known projects before use; falls back to parent if unrecognised
- main() uses each subtask's project_name when creating the idea in the API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.summary: Container App Job triggered by ideas-api. Clones the target repository, runs an AI-driven tool-use loop to implement the requested feature, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request on GitHub. The job scales to zero between invocations.
content.description: A containerised Python agent that autonomously implements feature ideas. Triggered on demand by ideas-api, it clones the target repo, runs a GPT-4o tool-use loop to read/write files and execute shell commands, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request.
features: App-level retry mechanism for GPT-4o API calls with up to 4 attempts and exponential backoff (60/120/300s) to handle sustained 429 rate limits
Sliding context window for GPT-4o tool-use loop now trims older exchanges beyond the last 14 to reduce token growth and further mitigate 429 risks
Decomposes complex ideas into 2–4 focused sub-tasks to avoid 429 rate limits, reducing token usage and API load
Reduced token caps for tool outputs and agent loops: bash output capped at 2500 chars, read_file capped at 4000 chars, and max_tokens reduced to 4096

architecture.overview: A containerised Python agent that autonomously implements feature ideas. Triggered on demand by ideas-api, it clones the target repo, performs a pre-flight clarity check, runs an AI-driven tool-use loop to read/write files and execute shell commands, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request.
keyPoints: GPT-4o tool-use loop now includes app-level retry mechanism with up to 4 attempts and exponential backoff (60/120/300s) for handling sustained 429 rate limits
Sliding context window trims older exchanges beyond the last 14 to bound token usage and reduce 429 risks
Idea decomposition mechanism splits complex ideas into 2–4 focused sub-tasks when necessary, blocking the parent idea until all sub-tasks complete
Token caps adjusted to reduce API load: bash output capped at 2500 chars, read_file capped at 4000 chars, and max_tokens reduced to 4096 in agent and repair loops

dataFlow.label: Container App Job starts — fresh container
Pre-flight clarity check · request clarification if needed
Resolve repository dynamically from Ideas API
Decompose complex ideas into sub-tasks if needed · block parent idea
sublines: Evaluate idea complexity · create 2–4 focused sub-tasks
File sub-tasks via Ideas API · update parent idea status

dataFlow.label: Clone target repo · read idea description
sublines: read files · write files · run shell commands

dataFlow.label: Create cross-repo dependency ideas if needed · block until dependencies complete
sublines: Fetch all projects · file dependency ideas
Re-trigger checks dependency statuses · unblock automatically

dataFlow.label: Commit changes · open draft PR on GitHub
Monitor GitHub Actions CI · auto-repair on failure
sublines: Poll PR checks every 30s until completion
Fetch failure logs · diagnose issues · push fixes
